items.text: Decisions recorded: each open fork resolved with a one-line rationale.
Plan: the iterations to reach the objective, each a binary DoD sketch + committed/optional tag.
The first execution iteration is concrete enough to open next.

handback: decisions.json: 7 entries (F1-F7). plan: P1 E1 move-engine-to-root + plugin skeleton, P2 E2 skill wrappers over verbs, P3 E3 README + install + clean-repo smoke test, P4 closeout. All committed. F3 settled by operator: engine moves to repo root, .lume/ is in-repo state only and outside the plugin. Next: open P1 (E1) as the first execution iteration.
self_review: All 7 forks resolved with rationale; F3 (engine to root, .lume not part of the plugin) is the operator's call, recorded as such. Plan is 3 execution slices + closeout, F3-gated first (largest blast radius), each behaviour-preserving against the 227 tests and our own loop. P1 is concrete enough to open next. One assumption flagged for execution, not papered over: the exact mechanism by which .lume/workstreams does not ship as plugin content (marketplace subpath vs accepted-as-inert-data) - to be confirmed in E1/E3, not assumed. No engine code touched this iteration.
